User.title: User
User.description: Represents a registered user of the Swapno Uran Prakashan bookstore, storing their core profile information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication and login.

Publisher.title: Publisher
Publisher.description: Represents a publisher featured in the bookstore, including details for the 'Photo Time' gallery.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Publisher entity.

name.description: The official name of the publisher.

description.description: A brief description or biography of the publisher.

logoUrl.description: URL to the publisher's logo image.

PublisherGalleryImage.title: PublisherGalleryImage
PublisherGalleryImage.description: Represents an image within a publisher's gallery for the 'Photo Time' section.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PublisherGalleryImage entity.

publisherId.description: Reference to the Publisher this image belongs to. (Relationship: Publisher 1:N PublisherGalleryImage)

imageUrl.description: URL to the image file in cloud storage.

caption.description: A short description or caption for the image.

Book.title: Book
Book.description: Represents a physical book available for purchase in the store.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Book entity.

author.description: The name of the book's author.

publisherId.description: Reference to the Publisher of this book. (Relationship: Publisher 1:N Book)

price.description: The selling price of the book.

coverImageUrl.description: URL to the book's cover image in cloud storage.

description.description: A detailed summary or synopsis of the book.

isbn.description: The International Standard Book Number (ISBN) of the book.

isFeatured.description: Indicates if the book should be highlighted in the 'Featured Books' section on the landing page.

isBestseller.description: Indicates if the book is a current bestseller and should be displayed in the 'Bestsellers' section.

Ebook.title: Ebook
Ebook.description: Represents a digital book (PDF) available for purchase and secure viewing.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Ebook entity.

author.description: The name of the ebook's author.

publisherId.description: Reference to the Publisher of this ebook. (Relationship: Publisher 1:N Ebook)

price.description: The selling price of the ebook.

coverImageUrl.description: URL to the ebook's cover image in cloud storage.

pdfFileStorageKey.description: A secure key or path to the ebook's PDF file in cloud storage. This is NOT a direct download URL.

description.description: A detailed summary or synopsis of the ebook.

isbn.description: The International Standard Book Number (ISBN) of the ebook.

Cart.title: Cart
Cart.description: Represents a user's active shopping cart, primarily for physical books.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Cart entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this cart. (Relationship: User 1:1 Cart)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the cart was initially created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp of the last modification to the cart.

CartItem.title: CartItem
CartItem.description: Represents a single item (physical book) within a user's shopping cart.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CartItem entity.

cartId.description: Reference to the Cart this item belongs to. (Relationship: Cart 1:N CartItem)

bookId.description: Reference to the physical Book added to the cart. (Relationship: Book 1:N CartItem)

quantity.description: The number of units of the physical book in the cart.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a completed purchase transaction by a user, containing details about the entire order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who placed the order. (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: Timestamp when the order was placed.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary amount of the order, including all items and any applicable taxes/shipping.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'pending', 'completed', 'shipped', 'delivered', 'cancelled').

paymentTransactionId.description: The transaction ID provided by the payment gateway (e.g., Razorpay, Stripe) for this order.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a single product (physical book or ebook) included within a completed order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the Order this item belongs to. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

bookId.description: Reference to the physical Book if this order item is a physical book. (Relationship: Book 1:N OrderItem)

ebookId.description: Reference to the Ebook if this order item is an ebook. (Relationship: Ebook 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The number of units of the physical book ordered. Not applicable for ebooks.

unitPrice.description: The price of the item at the time of purchase.

itemType.description: Indicates whether the item is a 'physicalBook' or an 'ebook'.

UserEbookAccess.title: UserEbookAccess
UserEbookAccess.description: Records a user's entitlement to view a specific ebook after purchase, enabling secure PDF access.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserEbookAccess entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who purchased the ebook. (Relationship: User 1:N UserEbookAccess)

ebookId.description: Reference to the Ebook that was purchased. (Relationship: Ebook 1:N UserEbookAccess)

purchaseDate.description: Timestamp when the ebook was purchased and access was granted.

accessExpiresAt.description: Optional timestamp indicating when the user's access to the ebook expires, if applicable.

isAccessActive.description: Indicates whether the user currently has active access to view the ebook.

BookRecommendation.title: BookRecommendation
BookRecommendation.description: Stores personalized book recommendations generated by the AI tool for a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BookRecommendation entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User for whom the recommendation is generated. (Relationship: User 1:N BookRecommendation)

recommendedBookId.description: Reference to a recommended physical Book. (Relationship: Book 1:N BookRecommendation)

recommendedEbookId.description: Reference to a recommended Ebook. (Relationship: Ebook 1:N BookRecommendation)

recommendationDate.description: Timestamp when the recommendation was generated.

reason.description: A brief explanation of why this book was recommended (e.g., 'Based on your past purchases').

score.description: A numerical score indicating the confidence or relevance of the recommendation.

definition.description: Stores core user profile information. Documents are accessible only by the user whose ID matches `{userId}` in the path.
Stores public details about publishers. All documents are publicly readable to facilitate browsing throughout the app.

params.name: publisherId
params.description: The unique identifier for a publisher.

definition.description: Stores images for a specific publisher's gallery in the 'Photo Time' section. Publicly readable. Includes denormalized 'publisherId' for authorization independence and direct querying.

params.name: publisherId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent publisher.

params.name: imageId
params.description: The unique identifier for a gallery image.

definition.description: Stores details for physical books available in the shop. All documents are publicly readable, enabling secure queries for featured or bestseller books.

params.name: bookId
params.description: The unique identifier for a book.

definition.description: Stores details for digital books (Ebooks). All documents are publicly readable for browsing. Secure PDF access is handled via '/users/{userId}/ebookAccess' records and storage rules.

params.name: ebookId
params.description: The unique identifier for an ebook.

definition.description: Stores the single active shopping cart for a user. Accessible only by the owner whose ID matches '{userId}' in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who owns this cart.

definition.description: Stores individual items within a user's shopping cart. Accessible only by the owner whose ID matches '{userId}' in the path. For full authorization independence, 'userId' should also be denormalized within each CartItem document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who owns this cart item.

params.name: cartItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a cart item.

definition.description: Stores a user's completed orders. Accessible only by the owner whose ID matches '{userId}' in the path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who placed this order.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for an order.

definition.description: Stores individual items purchased within a user's order. Accessible only by the owner whose ID matches '{userId}' in the path. For full authorization independence, 'userId' should also be denormalized within each OrderItem document.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who owns this order item.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for an order item.

definition.description: Records a user's entitlement to view a purchased ebook. Accessible only by the user whose ID matches '{userId}' in the path. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'ebookId' for direct querying and authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user who owns this access record.

params.name: userEbookAccessId
params.description: The unique identifier for the ebook access record.

definition.description: Stores personalized book recommendations generated for a user. Accessible only by the owner whose ID matches '{userId}' in the path. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user for whom the recommendation is generated.

params.name: recommendationId
params.description: The unique identifier for a book recommendation.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following the core principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. All entities are categorized into either public-facing collections or user-private, path-based collections.

**Authorization Independence:**
Crucially, hierarchical authorization dependencies (e.g., rules requiring `get()` calls to parent documents) are explicitly avoided. This is achieved by ensuring that authorization-relevant fields are denormalized into the documents themselves or are directly available in the path wildcards.

1.  **User-Owned Data:** For all user-specific data (profile, cart, orders, ebook access, recommendations), a path-based ownership model `'/users/{userId}/...'` is used. This immediately binds the data to `request.auth.uid`, making security rules simple and efficient (e.g., `allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId;`).
    *   For `CartItem` and `OrderItem`, while the `userId` is present in the parent path segment (`/users/{userId}/...`), for absolute authorization independence and to perfectly align with Mandate A ("If a subcollection's access depends on the parent's attributes (owner, members), those attributes MUST be copied into every document"), it is recommended that `userId` also be explicitly stored as a field within each `CartItem` and `OrderItem` document. This allows for atomic write operations and simplified, direct access control without relying on the parent document's existence or data.

2.  **Public Data:** Entities like `Publisher`, `Book`, and `Ebook` are stored in top-level collections (`/publishers`, `/books`, `/ebooks`). These collections are designed with a homogeneous security posture: all documents within them are publicly readable. This eliminates the need for complex filtering logic in rules and allows for straightforward `list` queries without security concerns.
    *   `PublisherGalleryImage` documents are placed as subcollections of `publishers` (`/publishers/{publisherId}/galleryImages/{imageId}`). This maintains logical hierarchy while still being publicly readable. The `publisherId` is denormalized within each `PublisherGalleryImage` document, enabling direct queries and authorization clarity.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

*   **Public Collections:** For `/books`, `/ebooks`, and `/publishers` (and their subcollections like `/publishers/{publisherId}/galleryImages`), `list` operations are inherently secure because all documents are intended for public consumption. Queries can filter by `isFeatured` or `isBestseller` on `/books` securely.
*   **User-Private Collections:** For paths under `/users/{userId}/...`, `list` operations are automatically scoped to the authenticated user's ID (`request.auth.uid`), guaranteeing that a user can only query their own data. This design effectively makes `list` operations secure by construction without needing complex filtering logic in rules.

**DBAC (Database Access Control):**
While not explicitly creating a 'roles' collection based on the given entities, the pattern for administrative access would involve an existence-based check, for example, a `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection where the mere presence of a document signifies admin privileges. This adheres to the principle of storing roles in the database and relying on `request.auth.uid` for authorization, avoiding custom claims.
